title: tithymalus arkansanus
intro: Tithymalus arkansanus, also known as the Arkansas Globe Mallow, is a rare succulent plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its sap, which contains toxic compounds called cardenolides, similar to those found in digitalis. The plant's sap has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and even as a treatment for snake bites. In herbal preparations, the sap of the Tithymalus arkansanus plant can be extracted and used in tinctures, salves, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
